name: product-ux
description: BeSpunky product & UX design skills. keep-users-oriented: whenever you make someone wait or move them through a process, answer the three questions - what is the expected result, where am I in the process, what is the next step - and pick the right feedback for deterministic vs nondeterministic processes. A universal service-design principle, expressed primarily through software UI (loading/progress, async ops, multi-step flows, long-running jobs, notifications, optimistic UI). astonishing-to-use: the co-equal force to the experience-design trio - a design must be astonishing to USE, not only to look at. The trio optimizes the look and feel (the art); this optimizes the USE (UX): is it effortless, understandable, free of hoops, respectful of attention, a joy in the hand - given how people REALLY hold their devices and feel while using them (thumbs, one-handed, walking, distracted, tired, bad signal). The use and the look are two co-equal forces that must refine each other in a PING-PONG, never a one-way check (concept -> ux -> reconcept -> ux, or ux -> concept -> ux); the mission sets who leads (utility/transactional/safety -> UX leads, art elevates but never taxes the use; brand/art/immersive -> concept leads then gets pressure-tested for ease; most -> back-and-forth) but it is ALWAYS a loop, and you never ship great beauty with mediocre use. Hard bar: never below great UX (architect-mentality refuse-false-tradeoffs - beautiful AND usable - and design-for-the-consumer). keep-users-oriented is one facet of this larger force. The four questions: is it easy? understandable? taking them through hoops / boring or annoying them? does the use delight? Tenets baked in: respond instantly and like real life (direct manipulation follows the finger); never hang the UX (heavy work async/background); load instantaneously (no/short-live splash, lazy + sized placeholders); smooth transitions everywhere; avoid abrupt layout shifts (CLS) at all costs; action -> fitting reaction; choose the lowest-friction input control; never make an important feature (high value, core to the app's concept/goal) depend on or require user-maintained state - derive/detect/default it instead, reserving user-set state for low-value, presentational, or bonus features (the exception is a tracking app, where logging IS the concept); think about and facilitate input of each data type, even inventing a control the world hasn't seen (our genius art); different layouts for different devices (not reflow); plan for device realities (the mobile keyboard). When validation or anything fails, be informative (what is wrong, why, how to fix - never just 'invalid') and carry the user to the problem (scroll into view / take-me-there, focus the control), never leaving them guessing. A router (friction & flow; facilitating input; clarity & cognitive load; errors & recovery; responsiveness & perceived speed; continuity & transitions; embodied & contextual use; reconciling art & use; joy of use). redesign-means-rethink: when asked to REDESIGN a UI (a layout, screen, page, component, or flow), treat it as a complete creative reconception FROM SCRATCH, never a modification or reskin of existing code - the existing implementation has ZERO design authority, do not read it to inform the new design or even look at it before conceiving the new one; reconceive the FORM from intent/requirements/feeling, honor the PURPOSE (what it is for, its data and functionality) which comes from the spec and the user not from reverse-engineering the old layout, and read the old code ONLY AFTER the new design exists to plan cleanup/overwrite/migration and confirm what functionality must survive, then build clean and remove the old. A targeted tweak (move a button, change a colour) is NOT a redesign. The anti-anchoring entry gate to the experience-design trio (envision -> stage-the-vision -> realize, run from scratch). envision-the-experience: imagine the world an interface lives in - its feeling, place, and metaphor - before any layout or component, and design every element as a native inhabitant of that world rather than a generic control on a page; the screen is a place, not a page. Interrogate every element the spec names (a 'menu' might become a sunflower whose petals you pick), speak in sensory/non-technical language naming no implementation, keep immersive from becoming busy (restraint over spectacle) - then LAND the world on the app: anchor every inhabitant of the world to the app's real anatomy (surfaces, flows, gestures, states), validate every transformed element against what the convention it replaced was buying (findability, scannability, thumb reach, safety) and against general app truths (one thumb, interruptions, long lists, empty/loading/error states), and produce a directable Vision (five beats incl. the anchor - the world-to-app map a stakeholder can point at and steer). stage-the-vision: the visual architect and creative MIDDLE of the trio - the Vision says what a screen should FEEL like but not what it should LOOK like, and grounding alone can't make ART (it only stops the generic/garish). This skill invents the bold, web-native artistic MOMENTS that turn the feeling into something you'd screenshot, staying at the ART level: it speaks the web's language (parallax, cinematic scroll, a character that turns to camera, a living background, type-as-image, reveal choreography) but describes what artful thing happens and how it feels, never how to build it (that's realize). The altitude ladder: envision speaks pure feeling (zero web words); stage-the-vision speaks web-native art ('the room is a parallax background that deepens on scroll; a lion's face fills the hero and turns to meet your eyes'); realize speaks engineering. Because a model isn't a native artist, it reaches art by inventing several BOLD concepts and choosing the striking-yet-true, STEALING from specific great work (award sites, real art) and adapting its moves, composing with craft (focal point, scale, negative space, the cinematic moment), and sourcing genuinely artful assets - all grounded so it's not generic, restrained so it's not garish (bold != loud), and judged by an OUTSIDE EYE for beauty (never self-certified). Produces the Staging (a bold concept + concrete described moments + a visual system), confirmed at low fidelity before high-fidelity build. Each moment is DECOMPOSED to physical truth (light, shadow, material, colour, depth, form, texture as they really behave - refusing the lone primitive that only symbolizes a phenomenon: warm light is never just a gradient) and staged across form factors, mobile-first (re-staged for a phone, not shrunk). Routes to a reference library (the web as an art medium; inventing the concept; decompose to physical truth - the painter's physics; composition & cinematic staging incl. staging across form factors; stealing from great work; grounding, restraint & the taste gate). realize-the-vision: the craftsman that turns a Vision AND a confirmed Staging into a real, running interface by RESEARCHING the truest, soundest means before writing any code - reads the Vision as a contract (the feeling must survive), surveys the field (GSAP, Motion, three.js/R3F/angular-three, Web Animations, CSS scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, Lottie/Rive, Canvas/SVG/WebGL, Web Audio, haptics), weighs tradeoffs and caveats (jank, layout thrash, reduced-motion, accessibility, mobile), and knows when to build in code vs. when to SOURCE an asset - figurative/photoreal art (a couple kissing, a landscape) is generated, licensed, or downloaded, never hand-coded into path-soup, handling licensing, review, and art-direction. Chooses deliberately for the vision AND the constraints, designs and confirms the architecture before building (architecture-first), wraps imperative libraries behind clean Angular seams, engineers each staged moment, and renders each decomposed phenomenon faithfully (coordinated layers under one coherent light model, a shader, or a sourced real asset - never a lone primitive like one gradient for warm light). Requires a confirmed Staging before high-fidelity build (else it invokes stage-the-vision first), never invents the art itself, never self-certifies aesthetics (an outside eye judges look), and verifies against the feeling and the Staging in the running app. Routes to a reference library (motion & timelines; depth/3D/spatial; scroll & cinematic; SVG/canvas/generative; build-vs-source & sourcing/generating assets; performance & budgets; responsive & adaptive layout - engineering each moment's per-form-factor staging, re-composing rather than shrinking; accessibility/reduced-motion/fallbacks; sound & haptics). The building member of the trio envision-the-experience -> stage-the-vision -> realize-the-vision (feeling -> Staging -> build).
